source_units.text_raw:
The intent of the Heavy Industrial Zone is to provide areas for a range of industrial, manufacturing, warehousing, logistics, and related employment uses that contribute to the economic development of the City. This zone is intended to accommodate activities that require larger sites, specialized infrastructure, and separation from sensitive land uses due to noise, traffic, or operational characteristics. Development within the Industrial Zone shall promote efficient land use, safe and effective transportation access, and compatibility with adjacent uses, while supporting innovation, job creation, and long-term economic sustainability.
Rezoning from HI to another zone, except BP, is not permitted.
The following uses are permitted in the HI Zone:
Fire Station
Police Station
Public Utility Service Operation
Automobile Body Shop
Automobile Sales and Services
Automobile Service Station
Equipment Sales, Rental Service
Office
Retail Store
Retail Store with connected Retail Warehouse, Light Manufacturing or assembly facility
Retail Warehouse
Service Repair Establishment
Transportation Service
Abattoir
Bulk Fuel Station
Extractive Facilities
Feedmill
Heavy Equipment Repair
Kennel
Manufacturing, Heavy
Manufacturing, Light
Marijuana Production Facility
Recycling Depot
Salvage or Waste Disposal Facility
Warehouse, Storage Facility and/or Distribution Centre
Forestry Related
Outdoor Storage
Transport Terminal
These uses are permitted so long as the following lot requirements are met:
(a) Lot Area min. 600m2 interior lot, 700m2 corner lot
(b) Frontage min. 20 m
(c) Front Yard Setback min. 7.5 m
(d) Flankage Yard Setback min. 7.5 m
(e) Side Yard Setback min. 3 m, 6 m if bordering on any residential zone
(f) Rear Yard Setback min. 10 m, 20 m if bordering on any residential zone
(g) Lot Coverage max. 35%
Accessory buildings are permitted according to Section 3.1
Permitted encroachments into the setbacks are listed in Table 3.2.
Refuelling stations may be permitted by Development Agreement.
Uses that produce strong odours or loud noises may be subject to additional studies (at the discretion of the Development Officer) to minimize impacts on surrounding land uses that may be negatively impacted;
Hazardous materials handling or processing, may be subject to provincial and federal approvals.
Two buildings per lot are permitted.
The maximum building height shall be 12 m or 3 storeys.
Loading bays and service doors shall be located to the side or rear of the building where abutting a public street except where the lot configuration makes it impossible to do so.
Building facades facing a public street shall include architectural articulation, variation in materials, or glazing.
Blank facades exceeding 20 m in length are prohibited along a public street.
Outdoor storage in the HI Zone shall:
Not be permitted in the front yard of the primary building;
Be fully screened in accordance with Section 5.6.2;
Not exceed the height of the principal building.
General landscaping in the HI Zone:
Shall not be less than 15% of the lot area and may include preserving existing woodlands or vegetation on site; 7.5 m Chain Link Fence 7.5 m, 20 m if bordering residential Min. Side Yard Setback 3 m; 6 m if bordering residential Must be buffered with landscaping when fronting directly on a street
May permit the use of planting beds, grassy berms, bio-swales, and stormwater features as part of the landscape calculation;
Requires native trees with a minimum height of 2.5 m at a rate of no less than 2 trees per acre.
Requires landscaped beds consisting of ornamental grasses, woody shrubs or perennials at a rate of no less than 0.5 m2 per metre of frontage of the principal building and located in the front yard.
Properties that abut residential uses shall include a 3 m wide landscape buffer along the abutting property line that includes:
A 1.8 m high (min.) opaque fence
One 60 mm caliper tree per 8 m of side or rear yard planted in the landscape buffer placed to block light and views.
For buildings that have been set back more than 3 m from the front lot line, no less than 1 tree shall be planted per front yard.
Any chain link fences fronting directly on a street must be buffered using a combination of trees, shrubs or vines.
All disturbed areas should be well landscaped using mostly (>75% quantities) indigenous, low maintenance plants that are well adapted for the local microclimates on each site. Shade tolerant species shall be specified for the north side of buildings or in areas of frequent shade. Similarly, windy sites should be planted with wind tolerant species. Disturbed areas cannot be left without landscaping.
Where grass is being considered for any project:
Ensure the grass species is selected is adequate for shade levels on the site.
Pegged sod is required on all landscaped slopes exceeding 30%. Erosion Control measures must be clearly identified on sloping sites to minimize soil erosion.
A minimum of 1.5 m wide of Sod strip is required adjoining any asphalt areas, pathways, gravel storage areas, or landscape beds.
A minimum of 2 m wide strip of sod is required for all drainage swales
Any reinstatement of public road right- of-ways must be sodded.
Hydraulic seeding is permitted in all other areas where sod is not required. Broadcast seed is not permitted unless used for spot repairs.
For any parking lots located in the front yard, a minimum 2 m wide hard surfaced sidewalk shall connect the front door of the building to any adjacent sidewalks adjoining the property if present.
Slopes shall be no steeper that three (3) horizontal to one (1) vertical, except as an engineered retaining structure. All slopes shall be vegetated (not necessarily grass) and no riprap stone armouring is permitted on any site unless it is not materially visible from any road or sidewalk.
All main entries should be well landscaped to a scale that is appropriate for the scale of the building and the importance of the entry sequence.
The front door of the primary building must connect to adjacent sidewalks and parking areas by a hard surfaced walkway no less than 1.8 m wide.
Parking is recommended in the side or rear yard of the lot, but any parking located in front yard of the primary building shall include one additional tree for every 10 parking spaces located in the front yard.
The development officer may forgo these landscape requirements if it can be demonstrated that any of these requirements do not apply on a specific site. In addition, they may require additional requirements on a site specific basis to deal with special site consequences.

review_flags.description: PDF text order and figure/table text were extracted with pypdf; verify section order, column flow, and page layout before normalization.
The zone contains table, figure, or schedule text. Values are preserved as extracted text and require source PDF layout review before normalization.
